name: Armando Peraza
abstract: Armando Peraza (May 30, 1924 – April 14, 2014) was a Cuban Latin jazz percussionist and a member of the rock band Santana. Peraza played congas, bongos, and timbales.

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: I just enjoyed working for Cal. And Carl Burnett was the drummer at the time. Armando Peraza. It was an education for me to play in the group...

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: And Peraza, Armando Peraza, played congo in Conjunto Kubavana[. So all these bands...it’s just amazing what was coming out of Cuba.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: When I finished with George Shearing, I went with Stan Kenton. Then Armando Peraza took my post with George Shearing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mario Bauza
about_quotes.evidence: That was my conga player... And then a guy name Armando [Peraza] came of (inaudible) in there for a while

about_quotes.speaker: George Shearing
about_quotes.doc_title: George Shearing interviewed by Joe Williams, New York City, New York, March 8, 1996
